targets.name: Sh2-129
targets.note: ~2.3 deg Ha shell; hosts the faint OIII 'Squid' Ou4. Premier Ha+OIII bicolor field.

targets.name: Ou4
targets.note: Near-pure OIII bipolar ~1.2 deg inside Sh2-129. Discovered 2011 (N. Outters). Very faint.

targets.name: Sh2-155
targets.note: Bright rim knot inside a larger complex; SHO friendly.

targets.name: Sh2-157
targets.note: Ha body with an OIII-responsive rim; frames with NGC 7538/bubble region.

targets.name: Sh2-132
targets.note: Strong SII as well as Ha -> excellent SHO subject; two WR stars inside.

targets.name: NGC 7822
targets.note: Large Ha complex with sculpted pillars; classic SHO field, high dec (circumpolar-ish).

targets.name: Sh2-101
targets.note: Compact bright Ha; Cygnus X-1 microquasar jet region nearby. Easy win.

targets.name: IC 1396
targets.note: Huge ~2.5 deg region; the trunk (IC1396A) is the highlight. Fills the field.

targets.name: Sh2-115
targets.note: Near Berkeley 90; pairs with PN Abell 71 (Sh2-116) in-field.

targets.name: Sh2-114
targets.note: Long very-dim Ha filament; deep wide-field Ha only.

targets.name: Sh2-119
targets.note: East of the North America Nebula; strong OIII-responsive ionization rim.

targets.name: Sh2-124
targets.note: Isolated ~0.75 deg region rich in Ha + SII; excellent SHO subject at your scale.

targets.name: Sh2-126
targets.note: >3 deg faint Ha complex over LBN 437; borderline wide-field, may want a 2-panel mosaic.

targets.name: Sh2-232
targets.note: Frames well as an Auriga mosaic with Sh2-231/233/235.

targets.name: Sh2-54
targets.note: Big ~2 deg Ha region containing NGC 6604; low southern culmination from your site.

targets.name: Sh2-82
targets.note: Small Ha knot with a blue reflection component; wants long FL, Ha+RGB better than pure NB.

targets.name: Sh2-86
targets.note: Bright Ha with NGC 6820/6823; classic ~40' SHO framing.

targets.name: Sh2-188
targets.note: Asymmetric bow-shock PN; bright Ha arc, secondary OIII. High dec.

targets.name: Sh2-240
targets.note: ~3 deg filamentary SNR; superb Ha+OIII but very faint, fills/exceeds the field.

targets.name: Cygnus Loop
targets.note: Whole loop ~3.8x2.7 deg; the entire Veil roughly fills your field for a single-frame classic.

targets.name: NGC 6960
targets.note: Bright OIII+Ha filament by 52 Cyg; easy, high-detail.

targets.name: NGC 6992
targets.note: Bright OIII+Ha; the other easy Veil win.

targets.name: CTB 1
targets.note: Ha shell with a striking OIII arc/blow-out; compact enough to frame well.

targets.name: G65.3+5.7
targets.note: Huge ~5 deg faint OIII+Ha remnant in Cygnus; mosaic/camera-lens object.

targets.name: Sh2-224
targets.note: ~1 deg faint remnant with distinctive 'wing'; rewarding Ha/OIII bicolor.

targets.name: HB9
targets.note: ~2.3 deg dual-band remnant; delicate Ha filaments + OIII arcs, fits the field.

targets.name: W63
targets.note: Cygnus-Loop-style faint arcs in Ha and OIII.

targets.name: G206.9+2.3
targets.note: Very faint diffuse Ha remnant in Monoceros; deep-Ha challenge.

targets.name: G156.2+5.7
targets.note: Thin OIII/Ha filaments; expert deep-NB object, high dec.

targets.name: W50
targets.note: Faint Ha shell around the SS 433 microquasar; wide-field Ha under dark skies.

targets.name: G205.5+0.5
targets.note: Huge ~3.7 deg very-low-SB Ha shell by the Rosette; wide-field Ha, exceeds the frame.

targets.name: G107.7-5.1
targets.note: ~2.5 deg delicate pure-OIII filament shell in Cassiopeia (Falls & Drechsler 2022-23).

targets.name: G209.9-8.2
targets.note: Nearly complete ~1.8 deg OIII shell near Ori/Mon border (Drechsler & Falls 2023).

targets.name: G210.5+1.3
targets.note: The set's strongest Ha/SII SNR candidate; easily framed SHO subject (Drechsler/Strottner/Falls 2023).

targets.name: Scylla
targets.note: Freshly announced 2025 Cepheus SNR (Drechsler/Sainty/Soto et al.); coords/size provisional.

targets.name: Pa 30
targets.note: Only imaged Type-Iax remnant; OIII shell + radial SII 'dandelion' filaments. Small, flagship OIII+SII.

targets.name: NGC 6888
targets.note: Bright Ha body with a strong OIII shell; textbook SHO/HOO target.

targets.name: Sh2-308
targets.note: Big spherical OIII WR shell; superb but low southern culmination from your site.

targets.name: NGC 2359
targets.note: Premier bicolor WR bubble; strong OIII with Ha/SII detail.

targets.name: WR 134
targets.note: One of Cygnus's finest OIII partial-ring/arc targets, centered on the WR star.

targets.name: Abell 31
targets.note: Large low-SB OIII PN; wide field ideal, good spring filler.

targets.name: Abell 33
targets.note: Clean blue OIII ring with a bright foreground 'diamond' star.

targets.name: Abell 39
targets.note: Near-perfect spherical OIII shell; small, wants long FL but a lovely HOO.

targets.name: Jones-Emberson 1
targets.note: Bright twin-lobe OIII in Lynx; excellent mid-wide OIII subject.

targets.name: HFG1
targets.note: Bow-shock PN: blue OIII arc + long red Ha trail. NOT Sh2-200. Pairs with Abell 6 in-field.

targets.name: Abell 21
targets.note: Large OIII+Ha crescent in Gemini; great wide-ish OIII target.

targets.name: Abell 6
targets.note: Round OIII shell in Cassiopeia; pairs with HFG1 in a wide frame.

targets.name: PuWe 1
targets.note: One of the largest/faintest PNe (~21'); big OIII arc, needs dark sky + long integration.

targets.name: K 1-16
targets.note: Very high excitation -> strong OIII / weak Ha; small, best at long FL.

targets.name: Sh2-71
targets.note: Bright bipolar in Aquila (Dec is +2.16, not -2). Ha-led with an OIII core.

targets.name: Ou 5
targets.note: Small bright bipolar OIII in Cygnus (Outters 2012); long FL only.

targets.name: Abell 72
targets.note: Compact OIII ring in Delphinus; moderate FL suits it.

targets.name: NGC 1360
targets.note: Big bright OIII PN; sits at your southern Dec edge (peaks ~40 deg) but a bright easy win.

targets.name: StDr 1
targets.note: Bright compact core + big faint Ha halo w/ OIII shell (Strottner & Drechsler 2019).

targets.name: StDr 4
targets.note: Rare SII-DOMINANT (shock-excited) PN -> a genuine SHO showcase; small trophy-detail target.

targets.name: StDr 16
targets.note: Small bright OIII+Ha PN; frame with surrounding Aquila Milky Way. Low southern dec.

targets.name: StDr 56
targets.note: Moon-sized (~0.7 deg) but extremely faint Ha+OIII; perfect scale for APS-C, needs 50-100h.

targets.name: StDr 99
targets.note: 17' faint OIII shell, circumpolar in Cepheus; weak OIII, genuinely hard.

targets.name: StDr 141
targets.note: Compact balanced OIII+Ha PN near M31; easy bicolor with M31-area context.

targets.name: StDr 161
targets.note: Elongated 10x5' Ha nebula in Cygnus with OIII detail; good summer wide-field target.

targets.name: Pa 5
targets.note: Compact fairly-bright OIII+Ha PN on the Lyra/Cygnus border (Patchick 2005).

targets.name: Pa 15
targets.note: Small GTC-confirmed OIII Patchick PN; bright-small, not wide-field.

targets.name: SDSO-1
targets.note: Among the faintest imageable structures (~100h). Pure OIII; frame the field around M31 + EG And, coords are an anchor.

targets.name: M31
targets.note: Huge; fits short-FL frames, core detail at longer FL. Add Ha for its HII knots.

targets.name: M33
targets.note: Big face-on spiral; superb Ha regions - LRGB+Ha.

targets.name: M51
targets.note: Classic interacting spiral; wants >=1000mm to fill frame.

targets.name: M81
targets.note: Bright grand-design spiral; pairs with M82 at short FL.

targets.name: M82
targets.note: Starburst edge-on; strong Ha outflow - LRGB+Ha.

targets.name: M101
targets.note: Large low-SB face-on; needs dark sky + long integration.

targets.name: M106
targets.note: Bright spiral with anomalous arms; add Ha.

targets.name: M94
targets.note: Bright compact spiral with outer ring.

targets.name: M74
targets.note: Grand-design face-on, low SB; add Ha.

targets.name: NGC 253
targets.note: Bright dusty starburst; sits at your southern edge - shoot at transit.

targets.name: NGC 7331
targets.note: Spiral with 'Deer Lick' companions; long FL.

targets.name: NGC 4631
targets.note: Edge-on with the Crowbar; add Ha.

targets.name: Leo Triplet
targets.note: Three galaxies in one field ~700-1000mm; a framing gem.

targets.name: Markarian's Chain
targets.note: Virgo galaxy string; wide-ish FL, many galaxies per frame.

targets.name: M22
targets.note: Huge bright globular; low south - shoot at transit.

targets.name: M45
targets.note: Bright stars + blue reflection nebulosity; short FL, LRGB (long L for dust).

targets.name: M35
targets.note: Large open cluster with NGC 2158 nearby.

targets.name: Double Cluster
targets.note: Twin clusters in one field; short-medium FL, gorgeous.

targets.name: M52
targets.note: Cassiopeia cluster; near the Bubble Nebula.

targets.name: NGC 7023
targets.note: Blue reflection nebula in dark dust; LRGB, long L.

targets.name: IC 2118
targets.note: Huge faint blue reflection; short FL, very dark sky.

targets.name: NGC 1333
targets.note: Reflection + dust in Perseus molecular cloud.

targets.name: Barnard 150
targets.note: Dark molecular filament; LRGB, long L, dark sky.

targets.name: LDN 1235
targets.note: Dark nebula 'shark'; wide-ish FL, very dark sky.

targets.name: M42
targets.note: The showpiece; huge dynamic range. SHO or LRGB+Ha. Low south - transit.

targets.name: M8
targets.note: Bright HII; low south - shoot at transit. SHO/HOO or LRGB+Ha.

targets.name: M27
targets.note: Bright big PN; HOO/SHO or LRGB+Ha; medium-long FL.

targets.name: M97
targets.note: Round PN; long FL; HOO. Pairs with M108.

targets.name: M1
targets.note: Bright SNR; SHO or LRGB+Ha; medium-long FL.

targets.name: NGC 7000
targets.note: Huge; short FL. SHO/HOO or Ha+RGB.

targets.name: IC 1848
targets.note: Pairs with the Heart; short FL; SHO.

targets.name: NGC 2264
targets.note: Cone + cluster + Fox Fur; medium FL; SHO.
